_comment: Mobile Resource Workbench budgets, keyed by device class × Eliza-1 tier. Mirrors the loadperf budgets/results/gate discipline. min* keys are throughput floors (measured value must be >= budget); max* keys are latency/memory/battery ceilings (measured <= budget). A null budget means 'no baseline measured yet' — recorded but never fails the gate; ratchet it in once a stable on-device baseline lands (see BASELINE.md). maxPeakRssMb / maxSteadyRssMb here are PROVISIONAL ceilings derived from each tier's GGUF footprint + runtime overhead and the device jetsam/foreground ceiling — they are limits, not measured values. Throughput, TTFT, and battery budgets are null until a physical-device baseline exists, because those cannot be honestly pre-filled. The rssLeak gate is always active.

ios-phone._note: iPhone tier (baseline device TBD per #8800 open question). Jetsam ceiling ~3-4 GB phys_footprint.

android-phone._note: Self-hosted arm64 device runner (Pixel-class per android-device-e2e.yml). Foreground-service memory ceiling is OEM-dependent. maxPostIdleUnloadRssMb gates the idle-reclaim workload (#11760): tail RSS after the inference idle-unload window must sit well below the warm footprint, or the unload policy has regressed and the app is back to being lmkd's standing target on 5.7 GB-class devices (which it kills at ~3.2 GB RSS per the #11506 forensics).

measuredBaselines._comment: REAL on-device captures, documented for reference (issue #11352). NOT consumed by the budget gate: the runner (run-workbench.mjs) reads only `deviceClasses.*` + `leakGrowthMbThreshold`, so nothing here can fail a PR. These are single-run, thermally-throttled captures — recorded per BASELINE.md discipline but intentionally NOT promoted into the gating `deviceClasses` budgets (which stay null) until multi-run stability on a quiet, cool device lands. Only keys with a genuine measurement are present; TTFT/battery/thermal/prefill are omitted because they were not honestly captured (power draw is power-meter-gated). Memory values are MB from `adb shell dumpsys meminfo <pkg>` (PSS/RSS); `glMtrackMb` is the GL mtrack line = Vulkan model weights resident in GPU memory. Throughput is decode tokens/sec from the bionic Vulkan host (GENERATE_STREAM), plus llama-bench on the same libs.

eliza-1-2b.device: Google Pixel 6a (Tensor GS101, Mali-G78 MP20, Android 16, ~5.7 GB usable RAM)
eliza-1-2b.model: eliza-1-2b Q4 (1.2 GB GGUF) via the bionic Vulkan host

ttftMs.note: Client-observed time to first SSE content chunk over 12 identical 294-char prompts (issue #11734). The mobile fast path delivers the whole reply as ONE chunk (bionic stream_next decodes the full 256-token buffer in a single step), so TTFT == full-turn latency on this build. 3/12 turns were transparent reloads after memory-policy releases (57.3-58.8 s); warm-only stats exclude them. Evidence: .github/issue-evidence/11734-pixel6a-adb-rows/.

prefillTokensPerSecond.note: 8-rung prompt-length ladder (92-219 exact Qwen3.5 tokens), regressing the device-logged in-lock work window: W = 32.4 s + 0.1945 s/tok * P (r^2 0.78). Batch-quantized, not linear: ELIZA_LLM_N_BATCH=128, so prompts <=127 tok sit flat (~53.7 s, decode-dominated) and each extra 128-token prefill batch adds ~23 s. Cross-check (same GGUF + Vulkan libs, app stopped): llama-bench pp128 3.88, pp512 8.86, tg128 6.78 tok/s. Effective in-app decode <= 7.9 tok/s (fit intercept). The historical decodeTokensPerSecond.warm=4.8 above is really 256 tok / (prefill + 256-token decode) - a combined window rate.

thermal.note: dumpsys thermalservice HAL temps + battery every 15 s across the whole #11734 session (probe + TTFT leg + prefill sweeps + 4b attempt). Thermal Status stayed 0 for the full 91 min - the OS throttling ladder never engaged under sustained ~55 s/turn Vulkan inference from a cool start. Battery level fell 76%->70% WHILE USB-powered (inference outdraws the adb-cable supply), then recharged; true discharge remains power-meter gated.

notMeasured.batteryDrainPct: power-meter gated (idle / chat / voice / background); tethered capture charges the device

findings: The eliza-1-2b E2B variant (4.9 GB) tripped lowmemorykiller on the 5.7 GB device; Q4 (1.2 GB) is the 6a-class tier.
Memory is dominated by resident weights: GL mtrack holds ~2.25 GB constant idle→active; per-turn delta is only ~100 MB PSS / ~70 MB RSS.

Measured idle total RSS (2390 MB) already exceeds the provisional `maxSteadyRssMb` cap (2300 MB) for this class — another reason not to naively promote provisional ceilings to a gate before multi-run baselines exist.

2026-07-03 (#11734): the on-device eliza-1-2b-q4.gguf header reports general.architecture=qwen35 (base Qwen/Qwen3.5-2B-Base), and the published bundles/{2b,4b}/text *-256k/-64k GGUFs are likewise Qwen3.5 — only the 8 GB *-128k files carry the Gemma-4 cutover. The fast path's Gemma-format prompt tags are plain text to this model, so decode always fills the native 256-token buffer (~46 s of the ~54 s turn) for a ~9-token reply.

eliza-1-4b.device: Google Pixel 6a (Tensor GS101, Mali-G78 MP20, Android 16, ~5.7 GB usable RAM)
eliza-1-4b.model: eliza-1-4b Q4_K_M (2.95 GB GGUF, bundles/4b/text/eliza-1-4b-256k.gguf, arch qwen35) via the bionic Vulkan host

notMeasured.decodeTokensPerSecond: no turn ever completed — the process was killed mid-load

findings: The 4b tier does not fit the 5.59 GB (5589 MB) device class: 2.95 GB of weights uploading to GPU (GL mtrack 3.22 GB) + app baseline crossed the ~3.2-3.4 GB foreground kill line ~24 s after boot; ApplicationExitInfo reason=3 (LOW_MEMORY) at RSS 3.4 GB with importance=100 (foreground). Matches the E2B-4.9GB finding above and the #11506 forensics.

The #11760 pressure-release policy cannot save an over-sized tier: the kill lands DURING the load, which holds the resident lock, before any release lever can fire. Android then auto-restarts the app, which would retry the load — a kill loop — until the model is swapped back.
The catalog's minRamGb: 6 floor for eliza-1-4b is confirmed real for this 5.59 GB device. Evidence: .github/issue-evidence/11734-pixel6a-adb-rows/ (leg 4).
